essence: Provides safe, reliable transport while navigating roads, people, and unpredictable city rhythms.

identity.title: Cab Driver / Taxi Driver
identity.summary: Transports passengers between locations, offering safe driving, local knowledge, and friendly customer service across varied routes and schedules.

salary.notes: Income varies widely depending on hours, location, licensing type (Hackney vs Private Hire), and whether the driver is self‑employed. Busy cities and night shifts can exceed £45k–£55k+.

qualifications: Private Hire or Hackney Carriage Licence (local authority)
Knowledge of local roads, traffic patterns, and navigation apps

lifestyle.description: Cab drivers spend their days (and often nights) transporting passengers across towns, cities, and airports. Tasks include accepting bookings, navigating routes, assisting passengers, handling payments, and maintaining a clean vehicle. The work offers independence and flexibility, but also long hours, unpredictable traffic, and the need for patience and strong customer service. Drivers encounter a wide range of people and situations, from quiet early‑morning airport runs to lively weekend nightlife. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy driving, autonomy, and meeting new people while staying calm under pressure.

habits: Keeps the vehicle clean and presentable
Plans routes and monitors traffic conditions
Manages fuel, maintenance, and daily logs
